task_completion.description: Open the specified base project file in TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application is open and the title bar or workspace indicates that 'base_project.top' has been loaded, displaying topographical contour lines.

task_completion.description: Draw a line intersecting at least 5 major contour lines to apply labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible line is drawn across the contour lines in the TopoCal workspace, clearly crossing at least 5 distinct major contour lines.

task_completion.description: Ensure numeric elevation labels are generated and visible on the contour lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Numeric values representing elevations (between 1600 and 2400) are visible along the contour lines where the intersection line was drawn.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting drawing as a DXF file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog box in TopoCal showing the DXF format selected and the file name 'labeled_contours.dxf' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal\' directory, or a file explorer confirming the file's creation.

task_completion.description: Save the TopoCal project to the specified TCL file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box in TopoCal showing the TCL format selected and the file name 'labeled_contours.tcl' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal\' directory, or a file explorer confirming the file's creation.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual TopoCal GUI rather than using terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the TopoCal application interface (with Spanish menus) being actively used to perform the annotations and exports.

integrity.description: The agent used TopoCal's built-in contour labeling tool rather than manually placing generic text boxes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The use of TopoCal's specific tool for annotating contours (via the Spanish menu interface) is visible, resulting in automatically aligned elevation numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create empty files or rename existing files to match the expected output names.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export and save actions are performed through TopoCal's native dialogs, and the workspace shows the actual labeled contours immediately before saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected DXF path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal\labeled_contours.dxf. Expected TCL path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TopoCal\labeled_contours.tcl. The agent must intersect at least 5 major contour lines. Elevation labels should be numeric values between 1600 and 2400. TopoCal uses a Spanish menu interface.
